_meta.purpose: Recorded snapshots of gap-extraction runs, replayed by ReplayExtractor so CI is deterministic and network-free. Keyed by curriculum slug so the harness can score gap detection across MULTIPLE curricula (RE3). Each list is crafted to exercise the metrics machinery with hand-computable precision/recall against that curriculum's planted gaps.
_meta.note: SYNTHETIC / portfolio demo artifact. NOT live model output.

per_curriculum_intended_metrics.agentic-ai-production: 2 TP (MCP, Agent Observability), 1 FN (moderate Fine-tuning gap not surfaced), 1 FP ('Kubernetes for Agents'). precision 2/3, recall 2/3. (Unchanged from the original single-curriculum fixture — preserves the default ReplayExtractor path + e2e test.)
per_curriculum_intended_metrics.cloud-platform-engineering: 4 findings hitting all 3 planted gaps (eBPF observability, Platform Engineering/IDP, FinOps) plus 1 FP ('Serverless Containers'). tp=3, fp=1, fn=0 -> precision 3/4, recall 1.0. NOTE: precision here is GAP-level (tp = distinct planted gaps hit, capped at the number of gaps), not finding-level, so 3/4 precision requires hitting 3 gaps. Deliberately DISTINCT from curriculum 1 (2/3 . 2/3) so the aggregate over both is a real micro-average, not a copy: tp=5, fp=2, fn=1 -> precision 5/7, recall 5/6.

evidence: Senior Agentic AI Engineer: 'A strong understanding of Model Context Protocol (MCP) for standardizing agent-tool integration is required.'
Multiple vendor docs reference MCP as the standard agent-tool transport.
agentic-ai-production.rationale: MCP appears across the corpus as a required agent-tool integration standard, but the bootcamp only teaches generic function-calling. A dedicated MCP module is warranted.

agentic-ai-production.topic: Agent Observability with OpenTelemetry & Distributed Tracing
evidence: Job postings list OpenTelemetry span emission and distributed tracing across multi-agent execution as production requirements.
Vendor docs describe LangSmith / Arize Phoenix trace ingestion for live agents.
agentic-ai-production.rationale: Production agent observability (OpenTelemetry, distributed tracing) is a first-class operational requirement in the corpus, distinct from the bootcamp's design-time offline evaluation.

evidence: A couple of job postings mention deploying agents on Kubernetes clusters.
agentic-ai-production.rationale: Some corpus entries reference Kubernetes-based agent deployment. (NOTE: this is a deliberately-planted FALSE POSITIVE — Kubernetes is not one of the ground-truth planted gaps; it demonstrates precision < 1.0.)

evidence: Multiple job postings standardize on eBPF observability with Cilium for kernel-level tracing across all clusters.
Vendor whitepaper: eBPF programs run safely in the kernel to capture network/security/performance telemetry without code changes.
cloud-platform-engineering.rationale: The program teaches Prometheus/Grafana metrics but not eBPF-based observability (Cilium, kernel-level tracing), which the corpus treats as a baseline production skill.

evidence: Vendor briefs describe FinOps platforms providing showback/chargeback reporting and cost-anomaly detection.
Roughly half the corpus lists cost governance and cost optimization as required or preferred skills.
cloud-platform-engineering.rationale: The program teaches 'Cloud Cost Basics' but not FinOps as a practice (cost governance, showback/chargeback). This is the lower-signal (moderate) gap — surfacing it demonstrates recall at a reduced signal level, distinct from curriculum 1 which MISSES its moderate gap.

evidence: Postings describe building an Internal Developer Platform (IDP) with Backstage golden paths and self-service infrastructure.
Platform engineering — treating the platform as a product — defines the dominant 2026 cloud-platform role.
cloud-platform-engineering.rationale: The program teaches Kubernetes/Terraform/GitOps as individual tools but not platform engineering as a discipline (IDP, Backstage, golden paths, platform-as-a-product).

evidence: A job posting mentions running some workloads on managed serverless container runtimes.
cloud-platform-engineering.rationale: Serverless-container runtimes get a passing mention. (NOTE: this is a deliberately-planted FALSE POSITIVE — it is not one of this curriculum's ground-truth planted gaps; it demonstrates precision < 1.0.)
